criteria.label: Recall and order the common growth classes from O(1) up to O(2ⁿ).
criteria.example: O(1) < O(log n) < O(n) < O(n log n) < O(n²) < O(2ⁿ) < O(n!)

criteria.label: Reduce a polynomial expression to its dominant Big-O term.
Distinguish worst, average and amortized analysis and say which applies to common Python operations.
criteria.example: `dict[k]` average O(1), worst O(n); `list.append` amortized O(1), worst O(n).

criteria.label: Spot accidental O(n²) patterns in code (nested loops, repeated `+=`, `in list` in a loop) and fix them.
criteria.example: Replace `if x in big_list` (O(n) each) with `if x in big_set` (O(1) each).

criteria.label: Use the doubling test to confirm complexity empirically.
criteria.example: Time the algorithm at n, 2n, 4n; if time doubles each step → O(n); if it quadruples → O(n²).

criteria.label: Pick algorithms and data structures based on Big-O to keep Gen AI pipelines scaling sub-quadratically.
criteria.example: Swap brute-force pairwise similarity (O(n²)) for an HNSW index (O(n log n) build, O(log n) query).
